input_class: Checkpointed architecture-gate report over report-first RMI architecture artifacts; imported as a static public-safe summary, not rerun from this repository.

source_artifact.sanitization_summary: The imported ASI-side fixture records gate names, pass bits, short evidence summaries, referenced report names, digest, residuals, and non-claims. It does not copy training rows, checkpoints, private traces, prompts, candidate payloads, or benchmark data.

redactions: No local report bodies beyond the architecture gate summary copied.

gate_decisions.evidence_summary: all architecture artifacts report zero external inference calls/violations

failed_attempts.reason: The local Project Theseus checkout contained modified and untracked files, including private-data surfaces, so this ASI Stack increment imports only a static sanitized report digest and does not claim a fresh live rerun.

residuals: The ASI Stack repository verifies the public-safe fixture and source-artifact digest; it does not run Project Theseus commands in CI.
The source checkout was dirty at import review, so the static report digest is evidence of a checkpointed report, not a current Theseus replay.
The architecture gate says heavy training is allowed inside Theseus under its own policy, but this book does not authorize, reproduce, or validate heavy training.
Chapter-core claims remain at argument until a separate evidence-transition record is accepted.

replay.verifier: ASI Stack validator checks schema, source digest, gate count, negative controls, redaction boundary, result digest, and non-claims.

claim_boundaries: This report can be cited as public-safe implementation-reference evidence that a Project Theseus checkpoint recorded a 14/14 architecture-gate pass with zero external inference calls.
This report cannot by itself promote governed self-improvement, readiness-gate, routing, benchmark, model-quality, safety, or ASI claims.
A future promotion would need a clean replay, vendored or archived public fixture, negative controls, and an accepted evidence-transition record.
non_claims: does not promote any chapter core claim above argument
does not prove deployed Theseus runtime behavior, model quality, routing quality, benchmark quality, safety, alignment, transfer, or ASI
does not authorize heavy training inside The ASI Stack book or reproduce the Project Theseus training pipeline
does not copy private training rows, checkpoints, dogfood traces, candidate payloads, prompts, or benchmark data into this public repository
does not replace future clean Project Theseus replay, external review, or chapter-core evidence-transition review
